pattern: A running streak of consecutive ascending steps lets every window's validity be answered with one lookup instead of rescanning it.
hints: A window is valid exactly when every adjacent pair inside it increases by exactly 1 - that's a property of the gaps between elements, not the window as a whole.
For each position, track how many consecutive prior gaps (nums[t] - nums[t-1] == 1) have held without a break; that streak length tells you the longest valid window ending there.
Precompute run[j] = length of the consecutive +1 streak ending at index j (run[0]=0). A window ending at index e with size k is valid iff run[e] >= k-1, and its power is nums[e] when valid, else -1.

edge_inputs.description: k=1, every single-element window is trivially valid
entire array is one ascending run
run breaks then restarts right at a window boundary

stress.description: random values near the upper bound, moderate k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [vals, k]

stress.description: long runs stitched together with random breaks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = []
    cur = rng.randint(1, 5)
    while len(nums) < n:
        run_len = rng.randint(1, 20)
        for _ in range(run_len):
            if len(nums) >= n:
                break
            nums.append(cur)
            cur += 1
        cur = rng.randint(1, 5)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
